lines.target: When France and England decided to build* a tunnel under the Channel, they asked for tenders.
lines.base: Cuando Francia e Inglaterra decidieron construir un túnel bajo el canal de la Mancha, solicitaron (por) presupuestos.
lines.pronunciation: .. bild & tanél andé... chanél... tenders 2 6 bi | de... ... .— ... ferme 1 .. dairektó. — 4... itss lisi... sed... — 7 ... mails ápaart! — 9 e caamli...

lines.target: The firm with the lowest offer was accepted. Astonished by the low price, they asked the director:
lines.base: Fue aceptada la empresa que presentó el más bajo. Admirados por el bajo precio, preguntaron al director:

lines.target: How are you going to do it for so little money?
It's easy, the engineer said, | will start digging* on the English side,
lines.base: Es fácil, dijo el técnico (ingeniero), empezaré a cavar en el lado inglés,
y mi hijo empezará a cavar en el lado francés,

lines.target: In that case, the engineer said* calmly, you will have two tunnels for the price of one.
lines.base: y nos encontraremos en el medio.
Pero eso es ridículo! Pasaréis (estaréis) a millas de distancia [uno de otro]
En ese caso, dijo el ingeniero tranquilamente, tendrán ustedes dos túneles por el precio de uno.

lines.target: A tourist in Cairo saw* two skulls in a shop: a large one and a small one.
lines.base: Un turista vió en El Cairo dos calaveras en una tienda: una grande y otra pequeña.
lines.pronunciation: .. kairo ... skéls... — 11 uotaar dzous 7... kuín Kliopátra... 12 ... néli... ameisd...

lines.target: What are those? he asked. — The big one is the skull of Queen Cleopatra, was the reply.
lines.base: ¿Qué es eso? (¿Qué son esas?) preguntó. — La grande es la calavera de la reina Cleopatra, fué la respuesta.

lines.target: Really, said* the amazed tourist, and the little one?
lines.base: Claro, dijo el atónito turista, ¿y la pequeña?

lines.target: That is the skull of Cleopatra when she was a young girl, answered the shopkeeper.
lines.base: Es la calavera de la reina Cleopatra, cuando era (una) joven, repondió el tendero.

lines.target: Don't forget” to repeat the irregular verbs we meet*.
lines.base: No se olvide de repetir los verbos irregulares.

gap_candidates.target: When France and England decided to build* a tunnel under the Channel, they asked for tenders.
The firm with the lowest offer was accepted. Astonished by the low price, they asked the director:
It's easy, the engineer said, | will start digging* on the English side, (2)
In that case, the engineer said* calmly, you will have two tunnels for the price of one.
A tourist in Cairo saw* two skulls in a shop: a large one and a small one.
What are those? he asked. — The big one is the skull of Queen Cleopatra, was the reply.
Really, said* the amazed tourist, and the little one?
That is the skull of Cleopatra when she was a young girl, answered the shopkeeper.
